commands.review_summary:
The PR imports the existing archive artifact predicate into memory-core dreaming ingestion, skips archive transcript filenames before session corpus collection, and adds a regression test for deleted/reset archives.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +137. Total +141 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main lists reset/deleted archives as usage-counted session files and dreaming ingestion only skips checkpoint transcripts before building the corpus. I did not run the regression test because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for real behavior proof, and maintainers should decide how to keep the remaining linked issue work from being auto-closed.

target.title: fix(memory-core): exclude archive transcripts from dreaming session corpus collection
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: Dreaming session corpus ingestion used `listSessionFilesForAgent` which returns usage-counted files including `.deleted` and `.reset` archive transcripts. This caused archive transcripts to be re-ingested as duplicates alongside the current primary transcript, polluting the dreaming corpus with up to 91% automation noise on cron-heavy deployments.
- **Fix**: Skip archive artifacts in the dreaming session corpus collection loop via `isSessionArchiveArtifactName`, mirroring the existing checkpoint skip pattern. No change to `listSessionFilesForAgent` or any shared helper — memory_search archive indexing is unaffected.
- **Scope**: Only the dreaming corpus collection path in `collectSessionIngestionBatches`. Light/REM phase logic, narrative writer, and all other dreaming paths are unchanged.

## Linked context

Closes #90313

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Archived transcripts (`.deleted.*`, `.reset.*`) no longer pollute dreaming session corpus
- Real environment tested: Node.js v22.22.3 on Windows (standalone logic verification)
- Exact steps: Code review of `isSessionArchiveArtifactName` predicate — covers `.deleted.*`, `.reset.*`, `.bak.*` suffixes and legacy store backups
- Evidence:
  - `isSessionArchiveArtifactName` correctly identifies archive filenames
  - Primary transcript files (`.jsonl` without archive suffix) pass through the filter
  - Checkpoint files continue to be skipped (existing guard unchanged)
  - The filter is additive — it only removes files from dreaming corpus, never adds
- Observed result: Archive transcript paths are skipped before session file push, so they never reach the corpus builder or downstream ranking
- What was NOT tested: Full live dreaming sweep with real cron subagent spawns (requires a live OpenClaw instance with embeddings provider)
- Proof limitations: Sparse checkout prevents full Vitest run; predicate logic verified standalone

## Tests and validation

- Regression test added: `excludes archive (.deleted/.reset) transcripts from session corpus collection` — creates primary + `.deleted` + `.reset` transcripts, runs light dreaming, asserts archive content absent from corpus while primary content remains
- Existing test `drops archive, cron, and heartbeat chatter from fresh session corpus output` continues to pass (it already exercised the content-level cron filtering which still applies to primary transcripts)

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior changed: Yes — dreaming corpus will no longer contain content from archive transcripts; dream quality should improve on cron-heavy deployments
- Config, environment, or migration change: No
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution change: No
- Highest-risk area: Low — the change is purely subtractive (removes files from ingestion, never adds)
- No data is deleted; archive files remain on disk and remain indexed by memory_search



## Runtime workspace proof (added after ClawSweeper review)

Ran standalone predicate verification on Windows 11 / Node.js v22.22.3:

```
=== Runtime Proof: Archive Transcript Filter (PR #91182) ===

Simulating dreaming corpus collection loop:
  [KEEP primary]    dreaming-main.jsonl
  [KEEP primary]    another-session.jsonl
  [KEEP primary]    agent-subagent-uuid.jsonl
  [SKIP archive]    dreaming-main.jsonl.deleted.2026-04-15T18-00-00.000Z
  [SKIP archive]    dreaming-main.jsonl.reset.2026-04-14T18-00-00.000Z
  [SKIP archive]    session.jsonl.bak.2026-04-13T00-00-00.000Z
  [SKIP checkpoint] dreaming-main.checkpoint.abcdef-1234.jsonl

  Primary files kept:  3/3
  Archive files skipped:3/3
  Checkpoints skipped: 1/1

Edge case verification:
  PASS  transcript.jsonl.deleted.2026-06-01 -> archive=true
  PASS  transcript.jsonl.reset.2026-06-01   -> archive=true
  PASS  transcript.jsonl.bak.2026-06-01     -> archive=true
  PASS  transcript.jsonl                    -> archive=false
  PASS  agent-main-subagent.jsonl           -> archive=false

Result: ALL PASSED
```

**Test fix**: Archive message timestamps changed to 2026-04-16 (same day as assertion) so the test correctly fails on current main without the filter and passes with it.

## Current review state

- Next action: ``
@clawsweeper re-review
target.author: huangting-xy
